mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits lower discount rates to equities and supports valuation expansion, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when the policy rate is in a clear easing trend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods where FEDFUNDS declines for at least two consecutive monthly observations, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income and consumer demand, which leads investors to price weaker near-term earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a 0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal loses negative sign consistency across two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both weaken together, the signal identifies broad household and construction demand rollover, which should depress cyclical earnings expectations and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS and HOUST are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns at a rate above 55%, or if either series alone explains the effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial throughput and stronger real activity, which supports earnings momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if breaks above the 3-month moving average in INDPRO are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the return edge disappears after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates stay unusually accommodative and equities should rerate higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above its recent trend and FEDFUNDS below 3% do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 marks accelerating real final demand, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 growth is not associated with positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of aligned observations, or if the sign flips in a sustained expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies labor-market deterioration that weakens consumption and corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly update
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the effect is no stronger than using either labor series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly rise in RSAFS signals improving nominal spending and revenue growth, which supports near-term equity performance and should push S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly RSAFS gains above 0.5% are not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal becomes neutral in periods of rising sales growth.
